Tests the multiplication of a boolean and a string resulting in a number.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the result of a type coercion operation in JavaScript. It multiplies the boolean value `true` by the string `"1"`, which JavaScript implicitly converts to the number `1`, and then verifies that the result is strictly equal to `1`. The use of the `assert` function indicates that this code is likely part of a test suite or a debugging process to ensure that type coercion behaves as expected in this specific scenario. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a single type coercion test case.
